title: Start housing before everything else
body: Treat housing as the critical path. Save screenshots, emails, and payment proof before sending money.

title: Keep one document folder
body: Carry scans and printed copies of your passport, admission letter, birth certificate, housing contract, and insurance documents.

title: Book municipality registration early
body: Municipality appointment slots can fill quickly around intake weeks, so book as soon as your address and arrival date are clear.

title: Keep IND messages together
body: Save IND letters and university immigration emails in one place because you may need them when collecting your permit.

title: Ask banks what they need
body: Requirements differ by bank. Some let you start online, but many still ask for ID, address details, and a BSN later.

title: Set up a reachable phone number
body: A Dutch or reliable EU phone number helps with two-factor authentication, deliveries, housing viewings, and government letters.

title: Look for a GP near your address
body: GP practices often register patients only inside their service area, so search around your postcode first.

title: Budget for wet-weather commuting
body: A lock, lights, rain gear, and basic repairs are part of daily bike life, not extras.

title: Leave buffer time on arrival day
body: Plan your first day around delays, luggage, key pickup, groceries, and finding your room rather than appointments.
